title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 2
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oo0K4ydn4rw

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into the first actual dungeon delve with Harlan the Barbarian and Saul the Witch Hunter. The quest to return the Dragon's Eye gem to Karak Azgal begins promisingly—an eerily quiet dungeon with no unexpected events for several turns. Then the bottomless pit appears, and everything goes sideways.

The witch hunter attempts to cross the rickety bridge, the planks give way, and Saul plummets into darkness. Daniel improvises a desperate leap test using the roleplay book's initiative rules—Saul needs a four or higher. He rolls a three. The witch hunter is dead. Harlan presses on alone, discovering a hidden room card and the Hall of Death before encountering three orcs in a guard room. Hopelessly outnumbered as a solo warrior with no chance of clearing the objective room alone, the barbarian faces the grim reality of Warhammer Quest's two-player scaling. The episode ends with combat about to begin and the campaign's future hanging by a thread.

----

What's your most memorable character death in Warhammer Quest—the one that came from pure bad luck rather than bad decisions?
